title: isoloma ceciliae
intro: Isoloma ceciliae, also known as 'Garden Isoloma', is a species of flowering plant in the family Aristolochiaceae. It is a perennial climber native to New Zealand, with heart-shaped leaves and small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Isoloma ceciliae are thought to include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and potential antimicrobial activity, although herbal preparations are not widely available or studied, and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
